design_philosophy.core_loop: Select plays, execute passes and shots, manage puck position, score goals while defending against opponent AI or human player
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time tactical hockey simulation with authentic move execution (one-timers, goalie control) versus opponent strategy
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Controlling an NHL team with authentic rosters and players from 1992-93 season, executing realistic hockey plays in televised broadcast-style competition
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to vertical camera perspective for strategic depth over contemporary side-view competitors; emphasis on authentic NHL mechanics (one-timers, manual goalie control) over arcade simplification; team-specific atmosphere through authentic organ songs and broadcast presentation

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects opponent team and difficulty through game mode selection (Exhibition, Playoffs, Best of Seven); relaxed game rules option available in Anniversary Mode

progression.unlock_mechanism: Game modes available from start: Exhibition Game, Stanley Cup Playoffs (Single Game), Stanley Cup Playoffs (Best of Seven), Shootout Mini-Game, Demo
replayability_hooks: All-star teams available alongside 30 official NHL rosters (including Mighty Ducks and Florida Panthers expansion teams)
User and player records can be saved
Multiple game modes (Playoffs, Best of Seven, Shootout) offer varied competitive structures
Authentic 1992-93 season rosters drive replay value through roster updates

world_building_techniques: Authentic NHL team rosters from 1992-93 season (1200+ real players)
Team-specific organ songs (e.g., Hartford Whalers' 'Brass Bonanza', Montreal Canadiens' 'Halte-là! Les Canadiens sont là!', St. Louis Blues' 'When the Saints Go Marching In')
Broadcast-style presentation with period-opening ceremonies and post-goal sequences
Real-life NHL footage in Sega CD version
Crowd meter and stadium atmosphere elements

faction_indicators: 30 distinct NHL teams with authentic rosters representing different franchises and regional identities
All-star teams representing player prestige and performance recognition
Expansion teams (Mighty Ducks, Florida Panthers) representing new market entry
danger_signals: Fighting removed from Sega CD version due to NHL restrictions, indicating regulatory/licensing constraints on violence representation
environmental_storytelling: Team-specific organ music creates distinct stadium identities and cultural association
Authentic player names and attributes encode real-world sports hierarchy and prestige
Broadcast-style presentation mirrors televised NHL experience, legitimizing competitive play
